rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes and implementation annexes in valid implementation scopes
Preserve the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections from the input AADL model
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
generic_rules: Ensure that the annex block is placed in the correct scope for the target component or implementation
Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers that are valid in the target scope
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Align the helper declaration type with the expression
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
